cards.title: Emotional Maturity: Raising Happy, Healthy Children
cards.body: Psychologist Lindsay C Gibson's bestselling book on emotionally immature parents offers practical advice for breaking unhealthy patterns. Her new work focuses on raising children with emotional resilience and healthy boundaries.

cards.title: Parenting Teenagers: The A-Level Challenge
cards.body: Parents of A-level students face unique frustrations trying to help with complex subjects they barely understand. The emotional support required during exam season can feel overwhelming and helpless.

cards.title: Vasectomy: Why More Men Should Consider It
cards.body: British men remain hesitant about vasectomies despite it being a reliable contraception option. Misinformation and fear online prevent couples from exploring this family planning choice.

cards.title: Why Families Need Space to Grow
cards.body: Living apart from family can trigger guilt, but healthy distance allows children, parents, and partners to develop individually. Maintaining connection while creating space strengthens long-term relationships.

cards.title: Fatherhood: Embracing Imperfection as Legacy
cards.body: Fathers launching adult children should accept their own past and present mistakes rather than hide them. This vulnerability creates a more loving intergenerational legacy for future generations.

cards.title: BFRB Treatment: Starting Before Willingness
cards.body: Children struggling with body-focused repetitive behaviors don't need to be ready to stop before treatment begins. Meaningful progress happens by addressing underlying causes before the child's motivation arrives.

cards.title: TikTok and YouTube Lack Child Safety Measures
cards.body: UK regulator Ofcom warns that major social platforms aren't protecting children adequately. Both platforms claim they've implemented safety features, but concerns about child exposure persist.

cards.title: Free Bus Trips for Children This Summer
cards.body: Chancellor Rachel Reeves announced free bus travel for children in England this August as part of cost-of-living relief. The measure aims to reduce family expenses during school holidays.

cards.title: Councils Funding Illegal Children's Homes
cards.body: Despite a ban on illegal children's homes, councils continue placing vulnerable children in unregulated facilities, spending up to £2m per child. The practice persists despite government restrictions.
